source.definition: Used to introduce a casual remark or an additional piece of information.

explanation.sourceText: The English phrase 'by the way' originates from the literal meaning of 'along the path' or 'incidentally', used to introduce something said in passing or as an aside.
explanation.targetText: Expresia engleză „by the way” provine din sensul literal „de-a lungul drumului” sau „întâmplător”, folosită pentru a introduce ceva spus în treacăt sau ca o remarcă laterală.

target.definition: Adverb folosit pentru a introduce o observație suplimentară sau o remarcă în treacăt.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'apropos' comes from the French 'à propos', meaning 'appropriate' or 'by the way'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „apropos” provine din franceza 'à propos', care înseamnă 'potrivit' sau 'apropos'.

examples.sourceText: By the way, have you heard the news yet?
I wanted to tell you something, by the way.
By the way, the weather will be better tomorrow.
examples.targetText: Apropos, vremea va fi mai bună mâine.

examples.sourceText: That is by the way very important to know.
examples.targetText: Asta este apropos foarte important de știut.

learningTips.text: Remember 'apropos' by thinking of the English phrase 'by the way' which is used to add extra information casually.
The Romanian 'apropos' comes from French, just like many English words, so it sounds familiar and easy to recall.
Think of 'apropos' as a polite way to 'propose' an additional comment in conversation.
When you say something 'by the way', you add something extra—imagine putting a little note on the side of your main message.
